#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Demo T7: full SimPoint pipeline (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7).
#
# Centerpiece demo showing every switching mode in the production
# SimPoint flow:
#
#   M4 - profile pass: QEMU+plugin bbv_out=,slice=N -> .bb file
#   ---  cluster      : tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint -loadFVFile ...
#   M5 - per-phase    : QEMU+plugin slice=N,slice_at=K,outfile= -> drain
#   M6 - archive      : write snap/K/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}
#   M7 - replay       : orchestrator replay-checkpoint --resume-with vsim
#
# Workload: rt_c_v_regs (C-level Tier-2 fixture with natural csrwi
# 0x7C0,1 exit marker, so replay's hardcoded vsim+csr trigger drains
# naturally without --hybrid-icount tuning).
#
# Env (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
#   VSIM_QEMU     qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN   libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_BIN      sim_<cpu>
#   VSIM_ELF      Tier-2 fixture (default: rt_c_v_regs.elf)
#   SIMPOINT_BIN  tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint
#   WORKDIR       persistent dir (default: mktemp -d)
#   SLICE         insns per BBV row / per slice (default: 200)
#   PROFILE_ICOUNT plugin icount fallback for profile pass (default: 8000)
#   PHASE_ICOUNT  plugin icount fallback per phase run (default: 8000)
#   MAXK          simpoint -maxK (default: 2)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
TOP_ROOT=$(cd -- "$REPO_ROOT/.." && pwd)

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.elf}"
SIMPOINT_BIN="${SIMPOINT_BIN:-$TOP_ROOT/tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint}"
WORKDIR="${WORKDIR:-$(mktemp -d -t demo-simpoint-XXXXXX)}"
SLICE="${SLICE:-20}"
PROFILE_ICOUNT="${PROFILE_ICOUNT:-2000}"
PHASE_ICOUNT="${PHASE_ICOUNT:-2000}"
MAXK="${MAXK:-2}"
# Defaults are tuned for rt_c_v_regs which reaches csrwi 0x7C0,0 after
# ~200-400 insns of QEMU1 startup. slice=20 gives ~15 BBV rows, enough
# for simpoint to find 2 clusters. Tune via env for other fixtures.

MODE_LABEL="Full SimPoint loop (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7)"
echo "=========================================================="
echo "Mode: $MODE_LABEL"
echo "Workload: $(basename "$VSIM_ELF")"
echo "Slice: $SLICE insns; profile icount: $PROFILE_ICOUNT; maxK: $MAXK"
echo "Workdir: $WORKDIR"
echo "=========================================================="

for f in "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then echo "[demo T7] SKIP: $f not built"; exit 77; fi
done
if [ ! -x "$SIMPOINT_BIN" ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] SKIP: $SIMPOINT_BIN not built"; exit 77
fi
if ! command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "[demo T7] SKIP: python3 not found"; exit 77
fi

mkdir -p "$WORKDIR"
BBV="$WORKDIR/profile.bb"
SIMPTS="$WORKDIR/simpts"
WEIGHTS="$WORKDIR/weights"
SP_LOG="$WORKDIR/simpoint.log"
SHM_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))

# ---- M4: profile pass ----------------------------------------------------
echo "[demo T7 1/4] M4 profile: bbv_out=,slice=$SLICE,icount=$PROFILE_ICOUNT"
SHM_PROFILE=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-t7-profile.XXXXXX)
PROFILE_STATE="$WORKDIR/profile_state.bin"
PROFILE_LOG="$WORKDIR/profile_qemu.log"
truncate -s "$SHM_SIZE" "$SHM_PROFILE"
trap 'rm -f "$SHM_PROFILE"' EXIT

set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHM_PROFILE,size=$SHM_SIZE" \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$PROFILE_STATE,bbv_out=$BBV,slice=$SLICE,icount=$PROFILE_ICOUNT" \
  >"$PROFILE_LOG" 2>&1
PRC=$?
set -e
rm -f "$SHM_PROFILE"

if [ "$PRC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] FAIL: profile QEMU expected exit 200, got $PRC"
  tail -20 "$PROFILE_LOG"; exit 1
fi
if [ ! -s "$BBV" ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] FAIL: BBV file empty"; exit 1
fi
BBV_ROWS=$(wc -l < "$BBV")
echo "  $BBV_ROWS BBV rows -> $BBV"

# ---- M4-cluster: simpoint -----------------------------------------------
echo "[demo T7 2/4] cluster: simpoint -maxK $MAXK -dim 2"
set +e
"$SIMPOINT_BIN" -loadFVFile "$BBV" -maxK "$MAXK" -dim 2 \
  -saveSimpoints "$SIMPTS" -saveSimpointWeights "$WEIGHTS" \
  >"$SP_LOG" 2>&1
SP_RC=$?
set -e
if [ "$SP_RC" -ne 0 ] || [ ! -s "$SIMPTS" ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] FAIL: simpoint rc=$SP_RC; simpts size=$(wc -c <"$SIMPTS" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)"
  tail -20 "$SP_LOG"; exit 1
fi
NUM_PHASES=$(wc -l <"$SIMPTS")
echo "  $NUM_PHASES cluster representatives:"
awk '{printf "    slice=%s cluster=%s\n", $1, $2}' "$SIMPTS"

# ---- M5+M6: per-phase drain + archive -----------------------------------
echo "[demo T7 3/4] for each phase: drain at slice_at=K + archive"
PHASE_INDICES=$(awk '{print $1}' "$SIMPTS")
declare -a ARCHIVES=()
for K in $PHASE_INDICES; do
  SHM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm "vsim-t7-phase${K}.XXXXXX")
  truncate -s "$SHM_SIZE" "$SHM"
  STATE="$WORKDIR/phase_${K}_state.bin"
  Q_LOG="$WORKDIR/phase_${K}_qemu.log"

  set +e
  timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
    -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHM,size=$SHM_SIZE" \
    -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
    -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
    -nographic \
    -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
    -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
    -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE,slice=$SLICE,slice_at=$K,icount=$PHASE_ICOUNT" \
    >"$Q_LOG" 2>&1

  set -e

  if [ "$QRC" -ne 200 ]; then
    rm -f "$SHM"
    echo "[demo T7] FAIL: phase $K QEMU exit $QRC"; tail -20 "$Q_LOG"; exit 1
  fi

  # M6: write snap/<K>/{state.bin, mem, meta.json} via the orchestrator's
  # write_archive helper (the canonical M6 entry point used by F9).
  SNAP="$WORKDIR/snap/$K"
  set +e
  PYTHONPATH="$TOP_ROOT/tools" python3 - <<PY
from pathlib import Path
from orchestrator.checkpoint import write_archive
write_archive(
    Path("$SNAP"),
    state_path=Path("$STATE"),
    mmap_path=Path("$SHM"),
    mmap_size=$SHM_SIZE,
    elf_path="verilator/hybrid/test/$(basename "$VSIM_ELF")",
    source_step_index=0,
    cpu_variant="ax45mpv_premium",
    region_id=f"snap/$K",
    slice_index=$K,
)
PY
  AR_RC=$?
  set -e
  rm -f "$SHM"
  if [ "$AR_RC" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "[demo T7] FAIL: write_archive for phase $K (rc=$AR_RC)"; exit 1
  fi
  for f in state.bin mem meta.json; do
    if [ ! -s "$SNAP/$f" ]; then
      echo "[demo T7] FAIL: $SNAP/$f missing/empty"; exit 1
    fi
  done
  ARCHIVES+=("$SNAP")
  echo "  phase $K archived -> $SNAP"
done

# ---- M7: replay each archive --------------------------------------------
echo "[demo T7 4/4] for each archive: replay-checkpoint --resume-with vsim"
REPLAY_OK=0
for SNAP in "${ARCHIVES[@]}"; do
  K=$(basename "$SNAP")
  REPLAY_WD="$WORKDIR/replay_$K"
  R_LOG="$WORKDIR/replay_${K}.log"
  mkdir -p "$REPLAY_WD"
  set +e
  PYTHONPATH="$TOP_ROOT/tools" VSIM_QEMU="$VSIM_QEMU" \
    VSIM_PLUGIN="$VSIM_PLUGIN" VSIM_BIN="$VSIM_BIN" \
    timeout 120 python3 -m orchestrator.main replay-checkpoint "$SNAP" \
      --resume-with vsim --project-root "$TOP_ROOT" --workdir "$REPLAY_WD" \
      >"$R_LOG" 2>&1
  R_RC=$?
  set -e
  if [ "$R_RC" -eq 0 ] || [ "$R_RC" -eq 200 ]; then
    echo "  phase $K: replay OK (rc=$R_RC)"
    REPLAY_OK=$((REPLAY_OK + 1))
  else
    echo "  phase $K: replay FAILED (rc=$R_RC)"
    tail -15 "$R_LOG"
  fi
done

if [ "$REPLAY_OK" -ne "$NUM_PHASES" ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] FAIL: $REPLAY_OK/$NUM_PHASES replays succeeded"
  exit 1
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Demo T7: full SimPoint pipeline (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7).

# Centerpiece demo showing every switching mode in the production

#   M4 - profile pass: QEMU+plugin bbv_out=,slice=N -> .bb file
#   ---  cluster      : tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint -loadFVFile ...

#   M6 - archive      : write snap/K/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}
#   M7 - replay       : orchestrator replay-checkpoint --resume-with vsim
#
# Workload: rt_c_v_regs (C-level Tier-2 fixture with natural csrwi
# 0x7C0,1 exit marker, so replay's hardcoded vsim+csr trigger drains
# naturally without --hybrid-icount tuning).
#
# Env (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
#   VSIM_QEMU     qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN   libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_BIN      sim_<cpu>
#   VSIM_ELF      Tier-2 fixture (default: rt_c_v_regs.elf)
#   SIMPOINT_BIN  tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint
#   WORKDIR       persistent dir (default: mktemp -d)
#   SLICE         insns per BBV row / per slice (default: 200)

#   PHASE_ICOUNT  plugin icount fallback per phase run (default: 8000)
#   MAXK          simpoint -maxK (default: 2)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
. "$SCRIPT_DIR/../lib/paths.sh"

FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-rt_c_v_regs}"
SIMPOINT_BIN="${SIMPOINT_BIN:-$TOP_ROOT/tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint}"
WORKDIR="${WORKDIR:-$(mktemp -d -t demo-simpoint-XXXXXX)}"
SLICE="${SLICE:-20}"
PROFILE_ICOUNT="${PROFILE_ICOUNT:-2000}"
PHASE_ICOUNT="${PHASE_ICOUNT:-2000}"
MAXK="${MAXK:-2}"
# Defaults are tuned for rt_c_v_regs which reaches csrwi 0x7C0,0 after
# ~200-400 insns of QEMU1 startup. slice=20 gives ~15 BBV rows, enough
# for simpoint to find 2 clusters. Tune via env for other fixtures.

MODE_LABEL="Full SimPoint loop (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7)"

echo "Workload: $(basename "$VSIM_ELF")"
echo "Slice: $SLICE insns; profile icount: $PROFILE_ICOUNT; maxK: $MAXK"
echo "Workdir: $WORKDIR"
echo "=========================================================="

for f in "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then echo "[demo T7] SKIP: $f not built"; exit 77; fi
done

  echo "[demo T7] SKIP: $SIMPOINT_BIN not built"; exit 77
fi
if ! command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "[demo T7] SKIP: python3 not found"; exit 77
fi

mkdir -p "$WORKDIR"
BBV="$WORKDIR/profile.bb"
SIMPTS="$WORKDIR/simpts"
WEIGHTS="$WORKDIR/weights"
SP_LOG="$WORKDIR/simpoint.log"
SHM_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))

# ---- M4: profile pass ----------------------------------------------------
echo "[demo T7 1/4] M4 profile: bbv_out=,slice=$SLICE,icount=$PROFILE_ICOUNT"
SHM_PROFILE=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-t7-profile.XXXXXX)
PROFILE_STATE="$WORKDIR/profile_state.bin"
PROFILE_LOG="$WORKDIR/profile_qemu.log"
truncate -s "$SHM_SIZE" "$SHM_PROFILE"
trap 'rm -f "$SHM_PROFILE"' EXIT

set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHM_PROFILE,size=$SHM_SIZE" \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$PROFILE_STATE,bbv_out=$BBV,slice=$SLICE,icount=$PROFILE_ICOUNT" \
  >"$PROFILE_LOG" 2>&1
PRC=$?
set -e
rm -f "$SHM_PROFILE"

if [ "$PRC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] FAIL: profile QEMU expected exit 200, got $PRC"
  tail -20 "$PROFILE_LOG"; exit 1
fi
if [ ! -s "$BBV" ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] FAIL: BBV file empty"; exit 1
fi
BBV_ROWS=$(wc -l < "$BBV")
echo "  $BBV_ROWS BBV rows -> $BBV"

# ---- M4-cluster: simpoint -----------------------------------------------
echo "[demo T7 2/4] cluster: simpoint -maxK $MAXK -dim 2"
set +e
"$SIMPOINT_BIN" -loadFVFile "$BBV" -maxK "$MAXK" -dim 2 \
  -saveSimpoints "$SIMPTS" -saveSimpointWeights "$WEIGHTS" \
  >"$SP_LOG" 2>&1
SP_RC=$?
set -e
if [ "$SP_RC" -ne 0 ] || [ ! -s "$SIMPTS" ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] FAIL: simpoint rc=$SP_RC; simpts size=$(wc -c <"$SIMPTS" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)"
  tail -20 "$SP_LOG"; exit 1
fi
NUM_PHASES=$(wc -l <"$SIMPTS")
echo "  $NUM_PHASES cluster representatives:"
awk '{printf "    slice=%s cluster=%s\n", $1, $2}' "$SIMPTS"

# ---- M5+M6: per-phase drain + archive -----------------------------------
echo "[demo T7 3/4] for each phase: drain at slice_at=K + archive"
PHASE_INDICES=$(awk '{print $1}' "$SIMPTS")
declare -a ARCHIVES=()
for K in $PHASE_INDICES; do

  Q_LOG="$WORKDIR/phase_${K}_qemu.log"

    -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHM,size=$SHM_SIZE" \
    -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
    -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
    -nographic \
    -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
    -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
    -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE,slice=$SLICE,slice_at=$K,icount=$PHASE_ICOUNT" \
    >"$Q_LOG" 2>&1
  QRC=$?
  set -e

  if [ "$QRC" -ne 200 ]; then
    rm -f "$SHM"
    echo "[demo T7] FAIL: phase $K QEMU exit $QRC"; tail -20 "$Q_LOG"; exit 1
  fi

  # M6: write snap/<K>/{state.bin, mem, meta.json} via the orchestrator's
  # write_archive helper (the canonical M6 entry point used by F9).
  SNAP="$WORKDIR/snap/$K"
  set +e
  PYTHONPATH="$TOP_ROOT/tools" python3 - <<PY
from pathlib import Path
from orchestrator.checkpoint import write_archive
write_archive(
    Path("$SNAP"),
    state_path=Path("$STATE"),
    mmap_path=Path("$SHM"),
    mmap_size=$SHM_SIZE,
    elf_path="verilator/hybrid/test/$(basename "$VSIM_ELF")",
    source_step_index=0,
    cpu_variant="ax45mpv_premium",
    region_id=f"snap/$K",
    slice_index=$K,
)
PY
  AR_RC=$?
  set -e
  rm -f "$SHM"
  if [ "$AR_RC" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "[demo T7] FAIL: write_archive for phase $K (rc=$AR_RC)"; exit 1
  fi
  for f in state.bin mem meta.json; do
    if [ ! -s "$SNAP/$f" ]; then
      echo "[demo T7] FAIL: $SNAP/$f missing/empty"; exit 1
    fi
  done
  ARCHIVES+=("$SNAP")
  echo "  phase $K archived -> $SNAP"
done

# ---- M7: replay each archive --------------------------------------------
echo "[demo T7 4/4] for each archive: replay-checkpoint --resume-with vsim"
REPLAY_OK=0
for SNAP in "${ARCHIVES[@]}"; do
  K=$(basename "$SNAP")
  REPLAY_WD="$WORKDIR/replay_$K"
  R_LOG="$WORKDIR/replay_${K}.log"
  mkdir -p "$REPLAY_WD"
  set +e
  PYTHONPATH="$TOP_ROOT/tools" VSIM_QEMU="$VSIM_QEMU" \
    VSIM_PLUGIN="$VSIM_PLUGIN" VSIM_BIN="$VSIM_BIN" \
    timeout 120 python3 -m orchestrator.main replay-checkpoint "$SNAP" \
      --resume-with vsim --project-root "$TOP_ROOT" --workdir "$REPLAY_WD" \
      >"$R_LOG" 2>&1
  R_RC=$?
  set -e
  if [ "$R_RC" -eq 0 ] || [ "$R_RC" -eq 200 ]; then
    echo "  phase $K: replay OK (rc=$R_RC)"
    REPLAY_OK=$((REPLAY_OK + 1))
  else
    echo "  phase $K: replay FAILED (rc=$R_RC)"
    tail -15 "$R_LOG"
  fi
done

if [ "$REPLAY_OK" -ne "$NUM_PHASES" ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] FAIL: $REPLAY_OK/$NUM_PHASES replays succeeded"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[demo T7] PASS ($MODE_LABEL): bbv_rows=$BBV_ROWS phases=$NUM_PHASES replays_ok=$REPLAY_OK workdir=$WORKDIR"

      echo "[demo T7] FAIL: $SNAP/$f missing/empty"; exit 1
    fi
  done
  ARCHIVES+=("$SNAP")
  echo "  phase $K archived -> $SNAP"
done

# ---- M7: replay each archive --------------------------------------------
echo "[demo T7 4/4] for each archive: replay-checkpoint --resume-with vsim"
REPLAY_OK=0
for SNAP in "${ARCHIVES[@]}"; do
  K=$(basename "$SNAP")
  REPLAY_WD="$WORKDIR/replay_$K"
  R_LOG="$WORKDIR/replay_${K}.log"
  mkdir -p "$REPLAY_WD"
  set +e
  PYTHONPATH="$TOP_ROOT/tools" VSIM_QEMU="$VSIM_QEMU" \
    VSIM_PLUGIN="$VSIM_PLUGIN" VSIM_BIN="$VSIM_BIN" \
    timeout 120 python3 -m orchestrator.main replay-checkpoint "$SNAP" \
      --resume-with vsim --project-root "$TOP_ROOT" --workdir "$REPLAY_WD" \
      >"$R_LOG" 2>&1
  R_RC=$?
  set -e
  if [ "$R_RC" -eq 0 ] || [ "$R_RC" -eq 200 ]; then
    echo "  phase $K: replay OK (rc=$R_RC)"
    REPLAY_OK=$((REPLAY_OK + 1))
  else
    echo "  phase $K: replay FAILED (rc=$R_RC)"
    tail -15 "$R_LOG"
  fi
done

if [ "$REPLAY_OK" -ne "$NUM_PHASES" ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] FAIL: $REPLAY_OK/$NUM_PHASES replays succeeded"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[demo T7] PASS ($MODE_LABEL): bbv_rows=$BBV_ROWS phases=$NUM_PHASES replays_ok=$REPLAY_OK workdir=$WORKDIR"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Demo T7: full SimPoint pipeline (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7).
#
# Centerpiece demo showing every switching mode in the production
# SimPoint flow:
#
#   M4 - profile pass: QEMU+plugin bbv_out=,slice=N -> .bb file
#   ---  cluster      : tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint -loadFVFile ...
#   M5 - per-phase    : QEMU+plugin slice=N,slice_at=K,outfile= -> drain
#   M6 - archive      : write snap/K/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}
#   M7 - replay       : orchestrator replay-checkpoint --resume-with vsim
#
# Workload: rt_c_v_regs (C-level Tier-2 fixture with natural csrwi
# 0x7C0,1 exit marker, so replay's hardcoded vsim+csr trigger drains
# naturally without --hybrid-icount tuning).
#
# Env (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
#   VSIM_QEMU     qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN   libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_BIN      sim_<cpu>
#   VSIM_ELF      Tier-2 fixture (default: rt_c_v_regs.elf)
#   SIMPOINT_BIN  tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint
#   WORKDIR       persistent dir (default: mktemp -d)
#   SLICE         insns per BBV row / per slice (default: 200)
#   PROFILE_ICOUNT plugin icount fallback for profile pass (default: 8000)
#   PHASE_ICOUNT  plugin icount fallback per phase run (default: 8000)
#   MAXK          simpoint -maxK (default: 2)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
. "$SCRIPT_DIR/../lib/paths.sh"

FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-rt_c_v_regs}"
SIMPOINT_BIN="${SIMPOINT_BIN:-$TOP_ROOT/tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint}"
WORKDIR="${WORKDIR:-$(mktemp -d -t demo-simpoint-XXXXXX)}"
SLICE="${SLICE:-20}"
PROFILE_ICOUNT="${PROFILE_ICOUNT:-2000}"
PHASE_ICOUNT="${PHASE_ICOUNT:-2000}"
MAXK="${MAXK:-2}"
# Defaults are tuned for rt_c_v_regs which reaches csrwi 0x7C0,0 after
# ~200-400 insns of QEMU1 startup. slice=20 gives ~15 BBV rows, enough
# for simpoint to find 2 clusters. Tune via env for other fixtures.

MODE_LABEL="Full SimPoint loop (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7)"
echo "=========================================================="
echo "Mode: $MODE_LABEL"
echo "Workload: $(basename "$VSIM_ELF")"
echo "Slice: $SLICE insns; profile icount: $PROFILE_ICOUNT; maxK: $MAXK"
echo "Workdir: $WORKDIR"
echo "=========================================================="

hybrid_resolve_paths
if [ ! -x "$SIMPOINT_BIN" ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] SKIP: $SIMPOINT_BIN not built"; exit 77
fi
if ! command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "[demo T7] SKIP: python3 not found"; exit 77
fi

mkdir -p "$WORKDIR"
BBV="$WORKDIR/profile.bb"
SIMPTS="$WORKDIR/simpts"
WEIGHTS="$WORKDIR/weights"
SP_LOG="$WORKDIR/simpoint.log"
SHM_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))

# ---- M4: profile pass ----------------------------------------------------
echo "[demo T7 1/4] M4 profile: bbv_out=,slice=$SLICE,icount=$PROFILE_ICOUNT"
SHM_PROFILE=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-t7-profile.XXXXXX)
PROFILE_STATE="$WORKDIR/profile_state.bin"
PROFILE_LOG="$WORKDIR/profile_qemu.log"
truncate -s "$SHM_SIZE" "$SHM_PROFILE"
trap 'rm -f "$SHM_PROFILE"' EXIT

set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHM_PROFILE,size=$SHM_SIZE" \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$PROFILE_STATE,bbv_out=$BBV,slice=$SLICE,icount=$PROFILE_ICOUNT" \
  >"$PROFILE_LOG" 2>&1
PRC=$?
set -e
rm -f "$SHM_PROFILE"

if [ "$PRC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] FAIL: profile QEMU expected exit 200, got $PRC"
  tail -20 "$PROFILE_LOG"; exit 1
fi
if [ ! -s "$BBV" ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] FAIL: BBV file empty"; exit 1
fi
BBV_ROWS=$(wc -l < "$BBV")
echo "  $BBV_ROWS BBV rows -> $BBV"

# ---- M4-cluster: simpoint -----------------------------------------------
echo "[demo T7 2/4] cluster: simpoint -maxK $MAXK -dim 2"
set +e
"$SIMPOINT_BIN" -loadFVFile "$BBV" -maxK "$MAXK" -dim 2 \
  -saveSimpoints "$SIMPTS" -saveSimpointWeights "$WEIGHTS" \
  >"$SP_LOG" 2>&1
SP_RC=$?
set -e
if [ "$SP_RC" -ne 0 ] || [ ! -s "$SIMPTS" ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] FAIL: simpoint rc=$SP_RC; simpts size=$(wc -c <"$SIMPTS" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)"
  tail -20 "$SP_LOG"; exit 1
fi
NUM_PHASES=$(wc -l <"$SIMPTS")
echo "  $NUM_PHASES cluster representatives:"
awk '{printf "    slice=%s cluster=%s\n", $1, $2}' "$SIMPTS"

# ---- M5+M6: per-phase drain + archive -----------------------------------
echo "[demo T7 3/4] for each phase: drain at slice_at=K + archive"
PHASE_INDICES=$(awk '{print $1}' "$SIMPTS")
declare -a ARCHIVES=()
for K in $PHASE_INDICES; do
  SHM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm "vsim-t7-phase${K}.XXXXXX")
  truncate -s "$SHM_SIZE" "$SHM"
  STATE="$WORKDIR/phase_${K}_state.bin"
  Q_LOG="$WORKDIR/phase_${K}_qemu.log"

  set +e
  timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
    -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHM,size=$SHM_SIZE" \
    -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
    -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
    -nographic \
    -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
    -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
    -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE,slice=$SLICE,slice_at=$K,icount=$PHASE_ICOUNT" \
    >"$Q_LOG" 2>&1
  QRC=$?
  set -e

  if [ "$QRC" -ne 200 ]; then
    rm -f "$SHM"
    echo "[demo T7] FAIL: phase $K QEMU exit $QRC"; tail -20 "$Q_LOG"; exit 1
  fi

  # M6: write snap/<K>/{state.bin, mem, meta.json} via the orchestrator's
  # write_archive helper (the canonical M6 entry point used by F9).
  SNAP="$WORKDIR/snap/$K"
  set +e
  PYTHONPATH="$TOP_ROOT/tools" python3 - <<PY
from pathlib import Path
from orchestrator.checkpoint import write_archive
write_archive(
    Path("$SNAP"),
    state_path=Path("$STATE"),
    mmap_path=Path("$SHM"),
    mmap_size=$SHM_SIZE,
    elf_path="tests/fixtures/$(basename "$VSIM_ELF")",
    source_step_index=0,
    cpu_variant="ax45mpv_premium",
    region_id=f"snap/$K",
    slice_index=$K,
)
PY
  AR_RC=$?
  set -e
  rm -f "$SHM"
  if [ "$AR_RC" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "[demo T7] FAIL: write_archive for phase $K (rc=$AR_RC)"; exit 1
  fi
  for f in state.bin mem meta.json; do
    if [ ! -s "$SNAP/$f" ]; then
      echo "[demo T7] FAIL: $SNAP/$f missing/empty"; exit 1
    fi
  done
  ARCHIVES+=("$SNAP")
  echo "  phase $K archived -> $SNAP"
done

# ---- M7: replay each archive --------------------------------------------
echo "[demo T7 4/4] for each archive: replay-checkpoint --resume-with vsim"
REPLAY_OK=0
for SNAP in "${ARCHIVES[@]}"; do
  K=$(basename "$SNAP")
  REPLAY_WD="$WORKDIR/replay_$K"
  R_LOG="$WORKDIR/replay_${K}.log"
  mkdir -p "$REPLAY_WD"
  set +e
  PYTHONPATH="$TOP_ROOT/tools" VSIM_QEMU="$VSIM_QEMU" \
    VSIM_PLUGIN="$VSIM_PLUGIN" VSIM_BIN="$VSIM_BIN" \
    timeout 120 python3 -m orchestrator.main replay-checkpoint "$SNAP" \
      --resume-with vsim --project-root "$TOP_ROOT" --workdir "$REPLAY_WD" \
      >"$R_LOG" 2>&1
  R_RC=$?
  set -e
  if [ "$R_RC" -eq 0 ] || [ "$R_RC" -eq 200 ]; then
    echo "  phase $K: replay OK (rc=$R_RC)"
    REPLAY_OK=$((REPLAY_OK + 1))
  else
    echo "  phase $K: replay FAILED (rc=$R_RC)"
    tail -15 "$R_LOG"
  fi
done

if [ "$REPLAY_OK" -ne "$NUM_PHASES" ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] FAIL: $REPLAY_OK/$NUM_PHASES replays succeeded"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[demo T7] PASS ($MODE_LABEL): bbv_rows=$BBV_ROWS phases=$NUM_PHASES replays_ok=$REPLAY_OK workdir=$WORKDIR"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Demo T7: full SimPoint pipeline (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7).
#
# Centerpiece demo showing every switching mode in the production
# SimPoint flow:
#
#   M4 - profile pass: QEMU+plugin bbv_out=,slice=N -> .bb file
#   ---  cluster      : tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint -loadFVFile ...
#   M5 - per-phase    : QEMU+plugin slice=N,slice_at=K,outfile= -> drain
#   M6 - archive      : write snap/K/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}
#   M7 - replay       : orchestrator replay-checkpoint --resume-with vsim
#
# Workload: rt_c_v_regs (C-level Tier-2 fixture with natural csrwi
# 0x7C0,1 exit marker, so replay's hardcoded vsim+csr trigger drains
# naturally without --hybrid-icount tuning).
#
# Env (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
#   VSIM_QEMU     qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN   libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_BIN      sim_<cpu>
#   VSIM_ELF      Tier-2 fixture (default: rt_c_v_regs.elf)
#   SIMPOINT_BIN  tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint
#   WORKDIR       persistent dir (default: mktemp -d)
#   SLICE         insns per BBV row / per slice (default: 200)
#   PROFILE_ICOUNT plugin icount fallback for profile pass (default: 8000)
#   PHASE_ICOUNT  plugin icount fallback per phase run (default: 8000)
#   MAXK          simpoint -maxK (default: 2)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
. "$SCRIPT_DIR/../lib/paths.sh"

FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-rt_c_v_regs}"
SIMPOINT_BIN="${SIMPOINT_BIN:-$TOP_ROOT/tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint}"
WORKDIR="${WORKDIR:-$(mktemp -d -t demo-simpoint-XXXXXX)}"
SLICE="${SLICE:-20}"
PROFILE_ICOUNT="${PROFILE_ICOUNT:-2000}"
PHASE_ICOUNT="${PHASE_ICOUNT:-2000}"
MAXK="${MAXK:-2}"
# Defaults are tuned for rt_c_v_regs which reaches csrwi 0x7C0,0 after
# ~200-400 insns of QEMU1 startup. slice=20 gives ~15 BBV rows, enough
# for simpoint to find 2 clusters. Tune via env for other fixtures.

MODE_LABEL="Full SimPoint loop (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7)"
echo "=========================================================="
echo "Mode: $MODE_LABEL"
echo "Workload: $(basename "$VSIM_ELF")"
echo "Slice: $SLICE insns; profile icount: $PROFILE_ICOUNT; maxK: $MAXK"
echo "Workdir: $WORKDIR"
echo "=========================================================="

if [ ! -x "$SIMPOINT_BIN" ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] SKIP: $SIMPOINT_BIN not built"; exit 77
fi
if ! command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "[demo T7] SKIP: python3 not found"; exit 77
fi

mkdir -p "$WORKDIR"
BBV="$WORKDIR/profile.bb"
SIMPTS="$WORKDIR/simpts"
WEIGHTS="$WORKDIR/weights"
SP_LOG="$WORKDIR/simpoint.log"
SHM_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))

# ---- M4: profile pass ----------------------------------------------------
echo "[demo T7 1/4] M4 profile: bbv_out=,slice=$SLICE,icount=$PROFILE_ICOUNT"
SHM_PROFILE=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-t7-profile.XXXXXX)
PROFILE_STATE="$WORKDIR/profile_state.bin"
PROFILE_LOG="$WORKDIR/profile_qemu.log"
truncate -s "$SHM_SIZE" "$SHM_PROFILE"
trap 'rm -f "$SHM_PROFILE"' EXIT

set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHM_PROFILE,size=$SHM_SIZE" \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$PROFILE_STATE,bbv_out=$BBV,slice=$SLICE,icount=$PROFILE_ICOUNT" \
  >"$PROFILE_LOG" 2>&1
PRC=$?
set -e
rm -f "$SHM_PROFILE"

if [ "$PRC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] FAIL: profile QEMU expected exit 200, got $PRC"
  tail -20 "$PROFILE_LOG"; exit 1
fi
if [ ! -s "$BBV" ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] FAIL: BBV file empty"; exit 1
fi
BBV_ROWS=$(wc -l < "$BBV")
echo "  $BBV_ROWS BBV rows -> $BBV"

# ---- M4-cluster: simpoint -----------------------------------------------
echo "[demo T7 2/4] cluster: simpoint -maxK $MAXK -dim 2"
set +e
"$SIMPOINT_BIN" -loadFVFile "$BBV" -maxK "$MAXK" -dim 2 \
  -saveSimpoints "$SIMPTS" -saveSimpointWeights "$WEIGHTS" \
  >"$SP_LOG" 2>&1
SP_RC=$?
set -e
if [ "$SP_RC" -ne 0 ] || [ ! -s "$SIMPTS" ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] FAIL: simpoint rc=$SP_RC; simpts size=$(wc -c <"$SIMPTS" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)"
  tail -20 "$SP_LOG"; exit 1
fi
NUM_PHASES=$(wc -l <"$SIMPTS")
echo "  $NUM_PHASES cluster representatives:"
awk '{printf "    slice=%s cluster=%s\n", $1, $2}' "$SIMPTS"

# ---- M5+M6: per-phase drain + archive -----------------------------------
echo "[demo T7 3/4] for each phase: drain at slice_at=K + archive"
PHASE_INDICES=$(awk '{print $1}' "$SIMPTS")
declare -a ARCHIVES=()
for K in $PHASE_INDICES; do
  SHM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm "vsim-t7-phase${K}.XXXXXX")
  truncate -s "$SHM_SIZE" "$SHM"
  STATE="$WORKDIR/phase_${K}_state.bin"
  Q_LOG="$WORKDIR/phase_${K}_qemu.log"

  set +e
  timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
    -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHM,size=$SHM_SIZE" \
    -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
    -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
    -nographic \
    -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
    -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
    -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE,slice=$SLICE,slice_at=$K,icount=$PHASE_ICOUNT" \
    >"$Q_LOG" 2>&1
  QRC=$?
  set -e

  if [ "$QRC" -ne 200 ]; then
    rm -f "$SHM"
    echo "[demo T7] FAIL: phase $K QEMU exit $QRC"; tail -20 "$Q_LOG"; exit 1
  fi

  # M6: write snap/<K>/{state.bin, mem, meta.json} via the orchestrator's
  # write_archive helper (the canonical M6 entry point used by F9).
  SNAP="$WORKDIR/snap/$K"
  set +e
  PYTHONPATH="$TOP_ROOT/tools" python3 - <<PY
from pathlib import Path
from orchestrator.checkpoint import write_archive
write_archive(
    Path("$SNAP"),
    state_path=Path("$STATE"),
    mmap_path=Path("$SHM"),
    mmap_size=$SHM_SIZE,
    elf_path="tests/fixtures/$(basename "$VSIM_ELF")",
    source_step_index=0,
    cpu_variant="ax45mpv_premium",
    region_id=f"snap/$K",
    slice_index=$K,
)
PY
  AR_RC=$?
  set -e
  rm -f "$SHM"
  if [ "$AR_RC" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "[demo T7] FAIL: write_archive for phase $K (rc=$AR_RC)"; exit 1
  fi
  for f in state.bin mem meta.json; do
    if [ ! -s "$SNAP/$f" ]; then
      echo "[demo T7] FAIL: $SNAP/$f missing/empty"; exit 1
    fi
  done
  ARCHIVES+=("$SNAP")
  echo "  phase $K archived -> $SNAP"
done

# ---- M7: replay each archive --------------------------------------------
echo "[demo T7 4/4] for each archive: replay-checkpoint --resume-with vsim"
REPLAY_OK=0
for SNAP in "${ARCHIVES[@]}"; do
  K=$(basename "$SNAP")
  REPLAY_WD="$WORKDIR/replay_$K"
  R_LOG="$WORKDIR/replay_${K}.log"
  mkdir -p "$REPLAY_WD"
  set +e
  PYTHONPATH="$TOP_ROOT/tools" VSIM_QEMU="$VSIM_QEMU" \
    VSIM_PLUGIN="$VSIM_PLUGIN" VSIM_BIN="$VSIM_BIN" \
    timeout 120 python3 -m orchestrator.main replay-checkpoint "$SNAP" \
      --resume-with vsim --project-root "$TOP_ROOT" --workdir "$REPLAY_WD" \
      >"$R_LOG" 2>&1
  R_RC=$?
  set -e
  if [ "$R_RC" -eq 0 ] || [ "$R_RC" -eq 200 ]; then
    echo "  phase $K: replay OK (rc=$R_RC)"
    REPLAY_OK=$((REPLAY_OK + 1))
  else
    echo "  phase $K: replay FAILED (rc=$R_RC)"
    tail -15 "$R_LOG"
  fi
done

if [ "$REPLAY_OK" -ne "$NUM_PHASES" ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] FAIL: $REPLAY_OK/$NUM_PHASES replays succeeded"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[demo T7] PASS ($MODE_LABEL): bbv_rows=$BBV_ROWS phases=$NUM_PHASES replays_ok=$REPLAY_OK workdir=$WORKDIR"

behavioral: rewire switching-mode demo scripts to top-level paths

the top-level repo.

the new layout e2e.sh lives one level up, so the call is now
"$SCRIPT_DIR/../e2e.sh".

The five orchestrator/QEMU demos source tests/lib/paths.sh and call
hybrid_resolve_paths with per-demo FIXTURE defaults (icount_spin for
M3/M4/M5; rt_c_v_regs for M6+M7 and T7).

demo_archive_replay.sh's plan YAML and demo_simpoint_loop.sh's
write_archive call had hardcoded 'verilator/hybrid/test/' ELF
references; both are updated to 'tests/fixtures/' so the orchestrator
resolves them under the new layout when --project-root points at the
top-level repo.

All seven demos verified PASS from the new location.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git log -1 --format="%h %s"

  - **F2d**: Apply the same RED/GREEN/REFACTOR cycle to `drivers/rt_*.sh`,
    `plugin/test_plugin_*.sh`, `plugin/probe_vcsr_drain.sh`, `demos/demo_*.sh`.
